# build123d easy: STL import

Wrap `build123d.importers` and import one tiny checked-in STL face.

## Why This Case Exists

The easiest build123d challenge should prove a real geometry import path without hiding the basic file contract behind a broad package scan.

## Case Shape

- ID: `build123d_easy`
- Family: `build123d`
- Difficulty: `easy`
- Tier: `gha_subset`
- Style: `package`
- Workflow Slice: `manufacturing`
- Target Kind: `package`
- Upstream Target: `build123d.importers`
- Catalog Source: `challenges/cases/build123d_easy/challenge.toml`

## Ingestion Breadth

- Minimum generated tools: `1`
- Required generated tools: `import_stl`

## Why This Stays A Challenge

This case keeps its ingestion recipe and workflow assertions in
`challenge.toml` instead of checking in companion `ingest.py` and
`use.py` scripts. That boundary is intentional: the challenge lane
should stay a compact problem statement rather than turning into a
worked answer key like the case-study lane.

## Run This Case

Use the convenience target:

```bash
make challenge CASE=build123d_easy
```

Or call the runner directly:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python scripts/run_challenges.py --catalog-dir challenges/cases --tier all --only build123d_easy
```

## Prepared Inputs

This case does not need rendered setup inputs.

## Fixtures

- `challenges/cases/build123d_easy/fixtures/tri.stl`

## Workflow

### 1. Import the tiny STL fixture

Tool: `import_stl`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "file_name": "{challenge_fixture_dir}/tri.stl"
}
```

Expectations: text contains ['Face object'].

## What This Case Proves

- One-shot ingestion can wrap a concrete CAD importer function from a focused submodule.
- The generated facade can drive a real file-based geometry import without hand-written glue.

## Challenge Definition

```toml
# Self-contained case: the smallest useful build123d importer workflow.
# This easy rung keeps the fixture tiny while still performing a real CAD import.
id = "build123d_easy"
title = "build123d easy: STL import"
tier = "gha_subset"
style = "package"
slice = "manufacturing"
family = "build123d"
difficulty = "easy"

[example]
summary = "Wrap `build123d.importers` and import one tiny checked-in STL face."
motivation = "The easiest build123d challenge should prove a real geometry import path without hiding the basic file contract behind a broad package scan."
proves = [
  "One-shot ingestion can wrap a concrete CAD importer function from a focused submodule.",
  "The generated facade can drive a real file-based geometry import without hand-written glue.",
]

[target]
kind = "package"
value = "build123d.importers"

[probe]
imports = ["build123d.importers"]

[scaffold]
kind = "package"
symbol_include_patterns = ["^import_stl$"]

[ingestion]
min_generated_tools = 1
required_tools = ["import_stl"]

[workflow]
[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Import the tiny STL fixture"
tool = "import_stl"
arguments = { file_name = "{challenge_fixture_dir}/tri.stl" }

[workflow.steps.expect]
text_contains = ["Face object"]
```
